# How SCIM works in HCP

This topic provides information about user and group lifecycles, SCIM statuses, and other concepts.

## Quotas and limits

To ensure optimal performance and prevent sync issues, HCP enforces quotas for SCIM provisioning. For current quota limits and to request increases, refer to the [HCP support page](/hcp/docs/hcp/admin/support).

## User and group statuses

When SCIM provisioning is enabled for your organization, HCP adds a **SCIM provisioning** column to the user and groups management pages in HCP. The **SCIM provisioning** column indicates whether the user or group is managed by the IdP over SCIM. Users and groups may have the following statuses:

| Status | Applies to | Definition |
| :----- | :--------- | :--------- |
| **Syncing** | Users and groups | SCIM is enabled and the user or group is synchronized with your identity provider |
| **Not enabled** | Users and groups | SCIM has not been enabled or has been disabled |
| **Non-SSO user** | Users | User accesses HCP with their email and password or GitHub credentials |
| **Created via SSO** | Users | User was created during sign-in through your SSO provider and is not managed by SCIM |
| **Created in HCP** | Groups | Group was created manually in HCP |

## Conflict handling

When you enable SCIM provisioning, HCP checks your identity provider directory to determine if users or groups already exist in HCP.

### Users

When SCIM is syncing, the lifecycle of SSO-based users are managed by your identity provider instead of HCP.

Users who are not provisioned through SCIM can still authenticate to your HCP organization. They are not rejected or removed.

### Groups

When a group with the same name exists in HCP and in your identity provider, the identity provider group takes precedence and replaces the existing HCP group. As a result, your identity provider manages the lifecycle of the pre-existing group.

When a group is replaced, the identity provider replaces all existing HCP group members with the members from your identity provider group.

## User and group deactivation and reactivation

When you deactivate or reactivate a user or group in your identity provider, the following behavior occurs in HCP.

### Deactivation

- The identity provider deletes the user or group from HCP
- All roles associated with that entity are removed

### Reactivation

- The identity provider creates the user or group in HCP as a new record
- You must reassign any roles for that entity in HCP
